name: Southern Fried Fish and Hush Puppies
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Small cleaned fish, preferably freshwater catfish or sunfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees F (93 degrees C).
2. In a stockpot or Dutch oven, pour oil to a depth of at least 1 1/2 inches and heat over medium-high until the temperature reaches 375 degrees.
3. Place wire racks on two baking sheets and set aside.
4. In a large bowl, mix corn flour, salt, pepper, and cayenne.
5. In a medium bowl, prepare the hush puppies batter: Whisk together egg and buttermilk, then stir in cornmeal until well blended. Add onion and stir again.
6. Place baking powder, salt, and baking soda in a small container and set aside while frying fish.
7. Dip each fish fillet into the seasoned flour, coating well but shaking off excess. Carefully lower each piece into the oil. Do not crowd the pot; the oil should bubble up around each piece.
8. Ensure the temperature stays between 365 and 375 degrees. Fry the fish until golden all over, about 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove fish pieces from the oil using a wire mesh strainer, allowing excess to drain. Place on prepared baking sheets when no longer dripping; return to oven.
9. For remaining hush puppies batter: Add reserved baking powder, salt, and baking soda to the batter, mixing well. Gradually add leftover seasoned corn flour until the batter is thick enough to spoon (about 1/4-1/2 cup more).
10. Return oil temperature to 375 degrees.
11. Drop spoonfuls of hush puppies batter into the oil using two teaspoons: one to scoop and one to scrape off and drop into the oil.
12. Fry hush puppies until golden all over, about 3 minutes. Drain each puppy well over the pot and place on prepared sheet pan.
13. Repeat process until all batter is fried.
